reports.title: UK Electricity Mix Weekly Report: Sunday 7 June
reports.intro: A measured weekly report for EnergyMix.info using the available 7-day historical generation feed for Monday 1 June to Saturday 6 June.
reports.summary: The week from Monday 1 June to Saturday 6 June averaged about 22.6 GW of measured generation, with renewables averaging 52.4% of measured output. The strongest renewable day in the available data was Thursday 4 June, when wind, solar and hydro together averaged 70.2% of measured generation. Gas was highest on Tuesday 2 June and lowest on Thursday 4 June.
reports.takeaway: For flexible electricity use, the cleanest opportunities are likely to appear when wind is strong, gas is low and demand is not at a peak. This is the foundation for future EV-charging guidance, clean-electricity alerts and weekly newsletter summaries.
drivers: Renewables were strongest on Thursday 4 June, when wind, solar and hydro averaged 70.2% of measured generation.
Wind was the largest swing factor in the available feed, peaking at about 11.9 GW average output on Thursday 4 June.
Solar was strongest on Friday 5 June, averaging about 3.7 GW across settlement periods after embedded-solar backfill where needed.
Gas averaged about 5.9 GW across the period, rising highest on Tuesday 2 June and falling lowest on Thursday 4 June.
Measured generation was highest on Monday 1 June, at roughly 24.0 GW average output.

cleanestPeriods.title: Best low-carbon proxy: Thursday 4 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Historical carbon-intensity aggregates are not yet part of this weekly report feed, so EnergyMix.info uses renewable share and gas output as cautious proxies. Thursday 4 June had the strongest renewable share at 70.2%.

cleanestPeriods.title: Lowest gas proxy: Thursday 4 June
cleanestPeriods.body: Gas generation averaged about 2.8 GW on Thursday 4 June. Lower gas periods are often cleaner, but the report does not yet claim this was the lowest-carbon period without historical carbon-intensity validation.

higherCarbonPeriods.title: Highest gas proxy: Tuesday 2 June
higherCarbonPeriods.body: Gas averaged about 10.1 GW on Tuesday 2 June. Gas-heavy periods are usually higher-carbon, but this is labelled as a proxy until historical carbon-intensity highs/lows are added to the report feed.

highlights.label: Wind
highlights.value: Highest average wind output was about 11.9 GW on Thursday 4 June.

highlights.label: Solar
highlights.value: Highest average solar output was about 3.7 GW on Friday 5 June.

highlights.label: Gas
highlights.value: Highest average gas output was about 10.1 GW on Tuesday 2 June; lowest was about 2.8 GW on Thursday 4 June.

reports.methodologyNote: This report is generated from the available 7-day historical generation feed and validated against external public data. Carbon-intensity highs/lows and interconnector summaries should be treated as future additions unless they are present as validated historical aggregates.

yesterday.title: UK Electricity Mix Yesterday: Saturday 6 June
yesterday.summary: Yesterday, Saturday 6 June, measured average generation was about 20.4 GW. Renewables averaged 58.4% of measured generation, gas averaged 3.8 GW, wind averaged 9.1 GW and solar averaged 2.1 GW.

highestRenewableShare.text: Current measured 7-day high: 70.2% average renewable share on Thursday 4 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestWindGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 11.9 GW average wind output on Thursday 4 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestSolarGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 3.7 GW average solar output on Friday 5 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

highestGasGeneration.text: Current measured 7-day high: about 10.1 GW average gas output on Tuesday 2 June, based on the available historical generation feed.

socialPosts.title: Monday weekly grid brief
socialPosts.body:
Last week in Britain’s electricity mix:

• Highest renewable share in the available feed: 70.2% on Thursday 4 June
• Highest average wind output: ~11.9 GW on Thursday 4 June
• Highest average solar output: ~3.7 GW on Friday 5 June
• Highest average gas output: ~10.1 GW on Tuesday 2 June

The shift was clear: gas-heavy weekdays gave way to a cleaner, wind-led weekend.

Full report: https://energymix.info/reports/weekly/2026-06-07/

socialPosts.title: Wednesday explainer
socialPosts.body:
What does “UK electricity mix” actually mean?

For live grid dashboards, it usually means Great Britain’s electricity system: England, Scotland and Wales. Northern Ireland is part of the UK, but operates in a separate electricity market.

Explainer: https://energymix.info/uk-electricity-mix

socialPosts.title: Friday practical clean-electricity post
socialPosts.body:
The cleanest time to use electricity is not a fixed hour.

It changes with wind, solar, demand, imports and gas generation. Windy overnight periods and sunny middays can be much cleaner than still early-evening peaks.

Guide: https://energymix.info/cleanest-time-to-use-electricity
